key_insight: Lone-pair-rich hybrid organic-inorganic perovskites can create a chemically soft lattice that suppresses phonon transport to achieve ultralow thermal conductivity.
engineering_relevance: This design rule offers a route to low-ba materials for thermoelectrics and thermal management without relying on nanostructuring or heavy disorder engineering.
method: Physics-guided symbolic regression to identify lone-pair-dominated compositions linked to low lattice ba
DFT plus electrical/impedance measurements to connect lattice softness, band structure, and transport
evidence_signal: The abstract reports room-temperature thermal conductivity of about 0.088 W m^-1 K^-1 for GuaPbI3, with bulk-dominated transport confirmed by impedance spectroscopy.
watch_item: The material is electronically heterogeneous and bias-dependent, so maintaining useful electronic transport while preserving ultralow ba may be challenging in devices.
paper_title: Lone-Pair-Induced Lattice Softness Enables Ultralow Thermal Conductivity in Hybrid Organic-Inorganic Perovskite GuaPbI$_3$
